metadata.name: MQoT v2 — Memory Quality Over Time
metadata.philosophy: Tests intelligent memory: store precisely, forget correctly, find accurately

scoring.retention: Important facts that SHOULD be remembered (repeated, emotional, specific)
scoring.forgetting: Noise/unknown that SHOULD be forgotten (abstaining = correct)
scoring.contradiction: Updated facts that SHOULD use new value

turns.content: Hey, can you help me find some inspiration for a logo I'm working on for a local bakery?
Absolutely! Can you tell me more about the bakery and the vibe they want?
It's called Sweet Flour, run by my friend Lena. They're in Capitol Hill, Seattle. They want something playful but not too childish.
Great details. Do they have signature colors or motifs they like?
They love pastel colors, especially light pink and mint green. Lena bakes these amazing croissants—those could be a motif.
Perfect! I'll gather some logo inspiration with pastel palettes and croissant elements. Anything else special about this project?
I'm really excited about this one because it's my first project since switching to Framer from Figma. The workflow feels smoother.
Congrats on the switch! That's a big move for a designer. Are you finding it easy to adjust?
Yeah, I used to be all about Figma, but Framer's prototyping is just next level. I committed to only using Framer for client work now.
Nice! I'll make sure to keep that in mind for future design questions. Let me know if you want some Framer-specific resources.
Morning! It's pouring rain in Seattle today, classic.
Typical Seattle weather! How are you feeling about the week ahead?
Pretty good, actually. I got my annual salary review yesterday—$95,000, up from $88,000. I'm stoked!
Congratulations, Jordan! That's a significant jump. How do you feel about your work these days?
Honestly, pretty motivated. My team at Lunar Creative is great, and my manager, Priya, has been really supportive.
Sounds like you're in a good place career-wise. Any plans for celebrating the raise?
I might treat myself to a new pair of running shoes. I’ve been running around Green Lake more lately.
That sounds like a fun reward! Let me know if you need help choosing a pair.
Will do. Oh, and Maya—my sister—called last night. She’s now the head nurse at her hospital in Portland!
Wow, double congratulations in your family! You and Maya both have big things happening.
Hey, do you know any good vegan restaurants in Seattle?
Sure! There’s Plum Bistro, Harvest Beat, and Wayward Vegan Café. Any particular cuisine you’re craving?
Not really, just trying to eat healthier. I used to go to Junkyard, but I’m trying to avoid fried stuff now.
Makes sense. Plum Bistro has some great fresh options.
Thanks! Also, I’m planning to visit Maya in Portland next weekend. She wants to show me her new office since she became head nurse.
That’s exciting! Are you taking the train or driving?
Driving. I might stop by Powell’s Books on the way—love that place.
Powell’s is a classic. Are you looking for anything specific?
Maybe something on branding. I want to up my game for freelance gigs.
I can recommend some great branding books if you want.
Please do! By the way, I had a quinoa bowl for lunch today—trying to keep it healthy.
Hi, any tips for presenting design concepts to a client who isn’t very tech-savvy?
Definitely. Use simple language, visual aids, and interactive prototypes. Are you preparing for a specific meeting?
Yeah, it’s for Lena at Sweet Flour. She’s more of a hands-on baker than a digital person.
Understandable. Maybe walk her through the Framer prototype step by step.
Good idea. I used to do static mockups, but now that I’m using Framer, I can show her real interactions.
That should really help her visualize the final product.
True. I’m kind of nervous though!
You’ve got this. Remember, you’re the expert.
Thanks. Oh, and Maya texted me a photo of her new head nurse badge. She’s so proud.
That’s so sweet. You two seem really close.
We are. I’m lucky to have her.
Afternoon! Any good music recommendations for working?
Sure! Do you prefer instrumental, electronic, or something else?
Usually lo-fi or ambient. I’ve been listening to Tycho a lot lately.
Tycho is great for focus. I can make a playlist if you want.
That’d be awesome. By the way, I’m prepping for the Seattle Half Marathon in November—it’s my first one.
Congrats on signing up! How’s the training going?
Pretty good. I run at Green Lake three times a week, usually after work.
Consistency is key. Let me know if you need a training plan.
Thanks! Also, I used to only run 5Ks, so this is a big step up for me.
You’ll do great! I’ll add some motivational tracks to your playlist.
Morning! The sun’s out for once.
Enjoy it while it lasts! Got any plans for the day?
Just work. Oh, and I’m heading to Portland tomorrow to see Maya. She’s giving me a tour of her hospital as head nurse.
Sounds like a special trip. Are you bringing her anything?
Yeah, I got her a personalized stethoscope as a congrats gift.
She’ll love that! Do you visit Portland often?
A few times a year. I used to go more, but work’s been busy.
Maybe you’ll have more time with your new raise. $95,000 gives you some freedom!
True! I’m thinking of saving up for a trip to Japan next spring.
That’s exciting. I can help you plan when the time comes.
Hey, I think I’m getting a cold. Any tips to feel better fast?
Rest, fluids, maybe some ginger tea. Do you have a busy week ahead?
Yeah, I have a big presentation at Lunar Creative. It’s for a tech startup called DynaLoop.
Hope you feel better soon! Is this the first startup you’ve worked with?
No, but DynaLoop is my biggest client so far. Their CEO, Marcus, is really particular about branding.
Sounds like a lot of pressure. How are you preparing?
I’m building the prototype in Framer—switched over from Figma a few weeks ago and haven’t looked back.
Glad the switch is working for you. Want any feedback on your presentation?
Yes, please! I’ll send you my deck tomorrow. For now, I need to grab a sandwich and rest a bit.
Take care, Jordan. Let me know if you need anything else.
Hi! The DynaLoop presentation went well. Marcus loved the Framer prototype.
That’s awesome news! How did you feel during the presentation?
Nervous at first, but once I started walking them through the interactive pieces, it clicked.
Your effort paid off. Did you get any feedback from your manager, Priya?
Yeah, she said I’m getting better at client communication. That means a lot to me.
You’ve made a lot of progress. Did you celebrate afterwards?
Sort of—I treated myself to a new pair of Brooks running shoes. I’m committed to my training for the half marathon.
Great choice! How’s your training going?
Good! I ran six miles yesterday, my longest yet.
Impressive! Keep it up, Jordan.
Evening! Quick question: any tips for keeping my houseplants alive?
Sure! What kind of plants do you have?
Mostly pothos and a fiddle leaf fig. The fig is struggling.
Fiddle leaf figs can be finicky. Try more indirect light and check soil moisture.
I’ll do that. I used to have a snake plant, but it died when I was traveling last fall.
Traveling can make plant care tough. Are you planning any trips soon?
Maybe Japan next year if I save enough from my new salary.
That would be an amazing adventure!
Yeah, I’m really looking forward to it.
Hey, random question: do you know any good co-working spaces in Seattle?
There’s The Riveter, WeWork, and Collective Chemistry. Are you looking to switch things up?
Yeah, working from home gets lonely. I’m thinking about joining Collective Chemistry.
That’s a creative space. Have you visited yet?
Not yet. I’ll check it out this weekend. Also, I used to go to coffee shops, but it’s just too noisy now.
A co-working space might be the right move then.
Agreed. Oh, and I started drinking black coffee instead of oat milk lattes. Needed a change.
Switching things up! How do you like black coffee so far?
It’s growing on me.
Hey, just a heads up—I switched from oat milk lattes to black coffee last month. I’m trying to cut back on sugar.
That’s a healthy switch. Do you miss the oat milk?
A little, but black coffee is simpler and less expensive.
Less sugar and fewer calories too!
Exactly. Also, I decided to join Collective Chemistry—signed up for a monthly membership.
Congrats! Hope you find it inspiring.
Thanks. I used to work from coffee shops, but Collective Chemistry has better Wi-Fi and fewer distractions.
Sounds like a good fit for focused work.
Definitely. I’m working on a new freelance project for a yoga studio, too.
Morning! Can you help me brainstorm some ideas for a yoga studio rebranding project?
Of course! What’s the studio called and what’s their vibe?
It’s called Rooted Flow. They want to attract more young professionals in downtown Seattle.
Great. Any color or style preferences?
They prefer earthy tones and minimalist logos. I’m excited because it’s my first freelance project since joining Collective Chemistry.
That’s awesome! How’s the space working for you so far?
Love it. I used to work at home and got distracted, but now I’m much more productive.
Glad to hear it. Want me to gather some branding inspiration for Rooted Flow?
Yes, please!
I’ll make a mood board for you.
Hey! Can you recommend a good place for a birthday dinner in Seattle?
Sure! Any preferences?
Something cozy, maybe Italian. I’m celebrating my 30th with friends—Maya’s coming up from Portland.
How exciting! Happy birthday in advance. How does Spinasse sound?
Spinasse is perfect. I’m really looking forward to it—first big celebration since my salary bump at Lunar Creative.
You deserve it! Are you planning anything else for your 30th?
Maybe a hike at Rattlesnake Ledge if the weather’s good.
Hope you have a great birthday, Jordan!
Hi, can you help me track my half marathon training? I want to hit 13.1 miles by November.
Absolutely. How many miles are you running currently per week?
About 15, split over three days. I used to only run 5Ks, so this is a big jump.
I’ll make a training schedule to gradually build your mileage.
Thank you! My new Brooks shoes are making a difference, too.
Glad you’re enjoying them. Any soreness or injuries so far?
No injuries. I’m careful about stretching.
Perfect. Stay consistent and you’ll be ready for race day.
Afternoon! Can you help me find a gift for my manager Priya? She’s celebrating her 5-year anniversary at Lunar Creative.
That’s thoughtful. What does Priya like?
She’s into books and tea. Maybe a gift card to Elliott Bay Book Company?
That’s a great idea. Do you want to include a personal note?
Yes, I’ll write something about how supportive she’s been—she helped me a lot during my last salary review.
She’ll appreciate that. Anything else planned for her celebration?
The team is taking her out for lunch at Din Tai Fung.
Sounds like a wonderful gesture.
